bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid. The opponent’s all-valueE offer is only about 0.25 utility for me despite their “balanced” message, so I trust the numbers rather than the cheap-talk framing and counter with my strongest package.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still the opening phase at t=0.02 and the opponent’s offers are low utility for me, with their latest “concession” on issueD actually reducing my utility. Their messages claim balance, but the offer history shows they are holding close to valueE-heavy packages, so I will make only a tiny concession on my lowest-impact tradeoff while keeping my key issues strong.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-maximum-utility bid while avoiding repeating my immediately previous offer. The opponent has made only small concessions, and their offers still give me low value on my highest-weight issues, especially issueA and issueB, so I will hold firm on those and only make a negligible adjustment on lower-priority issueE compared with my last bid.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only a tiny concession on the lowest-impact issueE. The opponent’s offers have improved slightly for me on issueC but remain very poor on issueA, issueB, and issueE, so I should not make major concessions yet; their “win-win” messages are not supported by the utilities of their bids.

At t=0.08 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a limited concession to avoid simply repeating my prior offer. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and their “win-win” messages are not supported by the numbers, but they consistently propose issueD around valueE/D, so I concede on low-weight issueD while holding firm on my highest-weight issues.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility offer while making concessions only on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and mostly anchored on valueE, so I am holding firm on my high-weight issues A, B, and C while offering valueE on lower-weight issues D and E to explore common ground.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making concessions only on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers and messages mostly favor valueE, with some movement on issueC/issueD, but they have not meaningfully conceded on my high-weight issueA or issueB, so I will hold those firm and vary my last bid by offering issueD: valueD and issueE: valueE.

I am targeting a still-strong utility of about 0.77 because it is early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers remain quite low for me despite their “balanced” language. Their bidding suggests they may care about pushing issueB/issueD/issueE toward valueE, so I am making a limited concession on issueB and issueD while holding firm on my high-value issueA and issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility bid around the upper-mid range because it is early at t=0.16 and the opponent’s offers remain low-value for me with only small, inconsistent concessions. Their messages repeatedly claim “balanced” and “win-win,” but the actual bids still hold firm on issueB and mostly unfavorable values, so I am conceding mainly on lower-weight issueD and moderately on issueB while holding issueA and issueC.

At t=0.18 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong utility for myself while making visible concessions on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s actual offers remain low-value for me despite their “win-win” messages, but they consistently signal interest in valueE/valueD patterns, so I am conceding on issueD and issueE while holding firm on my highest-value issueA and issueC.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.69 because it is early at t=0.20, so there is no need to make deep concessions yet. The opponent’s messages claim “balanced” offers, but the actual bids remain low for my utility and heavily favor valueE; however, they have occasionally moved on issueC and issueD, so I am holding firm on high-weight issueA/issueC while conceding a little on lower-weight issueD/issueE.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.70, since we are only at t=0.22 and there is no need to concede on my highest-value issueA or issueC yet. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and their “balanced” messages are not supported by the numbers, but they consistently signal interest in valueE/valueD on lower-weight issues, so I am conceding mainly on issueD and issueE while holding key priorities.

At t=0.24 there is still time, so I am targeting a relatively high-utility bid while continuing gradual concessions rather than moving to the opponent’s low-utility packages. The opponent’s offers show some movement on issueC and occasional movement on issueA, but they remain consistently poor for me on issueB and several low-weight issues; their “win-win” messages are cheap talk, so I rely on the bids. I am holding firm on my most important issueA and valuable issueC, while conceding more on lower-weight issueD/issueE and partly on issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.69 because it is only t=0.26, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s low-utility packages yet. The opponent’s offers and “win-win” language show only limited movement toward my priorities, especially on issueA and issueB, so I am holding firm on issueA and issueC while conceding on lower-weight issues and partially on issueB.

I targeted a still-strong utility around 0.65, conceding mainly on lower-weight issues and partially on issueB while holding my highest-priority issueA and issueC. At t=0.28 there is no need to collapse to the opponent’s package, but their latest offer shows movement on issueA and past offers show possible flexibility on issueC, so I am building around those signals while discounting their repeated “win-win” messaging as cheap talk.

I am targeting a solid but not maximal utility package (about 0.56), since at t=0.30 there is some time pressure but it is not yet late enough to accept the opponent’s low-utility packages. The opponent’s offers and messages suggest they are repeatedly pushing valueE/valueD on several issues, but they have signaled willingness to accept issueA:valueA once and issueC:valueD several times, so I will hold firm on high-importance issueA while conceding on lower or moderately costly issues.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.69, conceding mainly on lower-weight issues while holding firm on high-value issueA and issueC. The opponent’s latest offer improved substantially for me on issueB and issueC, so I am incorporating those signals but asking them to move to valueA on issueA, which is my most important issue. Their “balanced” messages are cheap talk, but the offer history shows some real movement worth reciprocating moderately at t=0.32.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.61, conceding mainly on lower-cost areas and on issueC where the opponent has repeatedly pushed alternatives, while still protecting high-importance issueA. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat but remain low for me overall, and their “balanced” messages are cheap talk, so I’m responding to the actual bidding pattern rather than the framing.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.69 by holding firm on my highest-value issues, especially issueA and issueC, while conceding mostly on lower-weight issueD/issueE and partially on issueB. At t=0.36 there is time left, so I should not drop to the opponent’s latest bid yet, but their recent offers show some real movement toward valueB on issueA/issueC and occasional acceptance of issueB valueB. Their “balanced/win-win” messages are cheap talk, so I am relying on the offer pattern rather than the wording.

I am targeting about 0.69 utility, keeping my highest-priority gains on issueA and issueC while making concessions on the lower-weight issueD and issueE. The opponent’s recent offers show real movement toward my preferred issueA=valueA and issueC=valueB, but they remain tough on issueB, so I am holding issueB at a moderate valueB rather than conceding further this early-mid stage.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.65: still protecting my high-weight priorities issueA and issueC, while conceding on lower-cost areas and on issueB where the opponent has repeatedly pushed toward valueD/valueE. At t=0.40 there is no need to drop to their latest low-utility package, but their history shows occasional movement to my preferred issueA/valueA and issueC/valueB, so I’ll anchor on those and make concessions on issueB, issueD, and issueE. Their “win-win” messages are noted but treated as cheap talk; the numerical offers matter more.
